title: A Clean Studio!
excerpt: When I took this photo, I was savoring the moment while the studio was clean. Taking bets on how long it will take me to mess it up! In fact, by the time this post goes live, I’m pretty sure…
body:
When I took this photo, I was savoring the moment while the studio was clean. Taking bets on how long it will take me to mess it up! In fact, by the time this post goes live, I’m pretty sure it’ll already look at least little less tidy. Mess is a natural part of having a crafty workspace (at least for me), but I did want to take a moment to show you some of the things I store on these shelves to keep things looking nice! 

Probably the first thing that grabbed your attention in this photo is my rainbow of Holsterbrands holsters filled with supplies. They make different sizes, holsters that can hold hot tools like curling wands (you've probably seen me take it on my trips in my vlogs), and of course they come in a bunch of colors!

I store my roller stamps on the Tim Holtz Ranger craft spinner. Unfortunately, this item was discontinued. Having a stand like this is really nice though, because I can turn it around to see the different stamps instead of having them tucked away in a drawer.

On the shelf, I’ve piled up a stack of copies of my book, Craft A Life You Love! It was picked up by Abrams, a big publishing house, and I am so excited to be finishing up on a new, expanded edition set to come out Spring 2008. Keep an eye out for that!

I’ve displayed the phrase, “Make it your own” on my Heidi Swapp mini light box.

You might know how much I love Orly nail polish, so it probably doesn’t surprise you that I keep them displayed on my shelf. They’re pretty and colorful, so why not?

I’m also really excited about my Stitch Happy sewing machine by We R Memory Keepers. Look at the light minty blue and the pretty, natural wood base. It’s just a stunningly beautiful machine and I can’t wait to start sewing with it! I am most excited about using twine. 

I hope you enjoyed this little tour of some of the organizers and other things I have displayed on my shelves to help me keep my studio tidy. Of course, messes are inevitable when crafts are involved, but I was happy to capture a moment where this section of my studio was neat.

I’d love to hear all about the ways that you keep your workspace organized! Do you have a particular strategy or maybe a product that helps you out? I’m looking forward to learning a few things from all of you. 

Shop HUSTLE & HEART now! Check your local retailer and online at Simon Says Stamp and Scrapbook.com

Disclosure: Some of the links above are affiliate links. I receive a small percentage of sales at no cost to you. My creative partners and I really appreciate your continued support and every little bit helps me to bring you consistent content!
